frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: noisy evidence accumulation to a termination bound on single trials

frameworks.name: population coding in LIP
frameworks.one_line: weighted sum of simultaneously recorded LIP neurons yields a scalar decision variable

core_question: Can the stochastic drift-diffusion signal underlying a single perceptual decision be directly observed in cortical population activity?
key_assumptions: LIP population activity reflects accumulating decision evidence
a single scalar variable captures the decision-relevant signal
deterministic drift and stochastic diffusion can be linearly decoded
manipulations: direction of random dot motion judged by monkeys
simultaneous recording from hundreds of LIP neurons
key_findings: a weighted sum of LIP population activity yields a scalar decision variable on single trials
this scalar combines a deterministic drift component with a stochastic diffusion component
the recovered single-trial signal aligns with predictions from drift-diffusion theory
interpretations: the long-hypothesized stochastic accumulation underlying choice variability is directly visible in cortex on single decisions
trial-averaged ramping reflects a true single-trial drift-diffusion process rather than an artifact of averaging
